query: Hi there, I have questions about this environmental testing report.
reference: Hello! I can help with that. I have access to the SpaceX TPDES permit application and environmental testing report for the Starbase Launch Pad Site in Brownsville, Texas (Proposed Permit No. WQ0005462000). What would you like to know?

query: Provide a high-level summary of the document.

tool_input.query: summary of the SpaceX TPDES permit application Starbase Launch Pad Site

reference: This is a Texas Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (TPDES) permit application package (Proposed Permit No. WQ0005462000) submitted by Space Exploration Technologies Corp. (SpaceX) to the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) on July 1, 2024. SpaceX operates the Starbase Launch Pad Site (RN111606745) at 1 Rocket Road, Brownsville, Cameron County, Texas 78521 — a site for Starship/Super Heavy rocket launch activity. The application seeks authorization to discharge non-process deluge system water used during launch operations via Outfalls 001 and 002 to tidal wetlands, thence to Rio Grande Tidal. The document is bilingual (English and Spanish) and includes administrative reports, technical worksheets, facility maps, a water-balance flow diagram, pollutant analysis from grab samples collected May 29 – June 6, 2024, and SPL Inc. laboratory reports with chain-of-custody records. [Citation: Administrative Package Cover Page; Technical Report 1.0, Item 1]

query: What does the report say about mercury levels?

tool_input.query: mercury levels concentration Outfall 001 sampling results

reference: Table 2 for Outfall 001 (grab samples, May–June 2024) reports total mercury concentrations of 113 µg/L in Sample 1 and 0.139 µg/L in Sample 2. The Maximum Analytical Level (MAL) for total mercury is 0.005 / 0.0005 µg/L. Sample 1 therefore substantially exceeds the MAL. The stormwater Table 17 for Outfall 001 also lists a mercury grab-sample maximum of <0.113 mg/L against a MAL of 0.000005 mg/L. [Citation: Worksheet 2.0, Table 2 for Outfall No. 001; Table 17 for Outfall No. 001]

query: List the Table 1 values for all pollutants at Outfall 001.

tool_input.query: Table 1 pollutant values Outfall 001 grab samples mg/L

reference:

query: Describe the water cycle—from source to Outfall 001—as detailed in the PDF.

tool_input.query: water balance flow diagram source water deluge launch mount outfall retention pond

reference: According to the water-balance flow diagram (Attachment J) and Technical Report 1.0: Source water (potable, raw, or Type 1 reclaimed water) is drawn and fills water storage tanks located at the launch pad. The water moves via piping to the deluge system at the Launch Mount. During launch operations, the deluge is activated and the water contacts the Launch Mount. The majority of this water is captured in Retention Basins 1 and 2, along with stormwater. Captured water is reused and recycled back into the tanks for future deluge operations. Minor amounts of deluge water that are not captured by the containment area discharge intermittently and at variable flow via Outfall 001 directly to tidal wetlands, and thence to Rio Grande Tidal. Sedimentation controls are applied to prepare water for reuse. [Citation: Attachment J — Water Balance; Technical Report 1.0, Items 1b and 2a]

query: Which outfall sampling point is closest to the Vertical Integration Tower?

tool_input.query: Vertical Integration Tower outfall sampling point location site map facility map

reference: According to the Site Map (Attachment H), the Vertical Integration Tower (VIT) is located on the northeastern portion of the SpaceX property, near Starship Test Stand B. Outfall/Sampling Point 2 (Outfall 002), associated with Retention Basin 2, is positioned on the eastern side of the facility — closer to the VIT than Outfall/Sampling Point 1 (Outfall 001), which is associated with Retention Basin 1 further to the west. Note that the site maps are image-based; exact distances cannot be extracted from the document text. [Citation: Attachment H — Facility Map and Site Map]

query: Which SpaceX employees listed in the document appear in the chain-of-custody records?

tool_input.query: SpaceX employees chain of custody records sample submitter personnel

reference: The chain-of-custody (CoC) records within the SPL Inc. laboratory reports (Project Nos. 1105141 and 1106094) list Rodolfo Longoria of Space Exploration Technologies (SpaceX), 1 Rocket Rd., Brownsville, TX 78521, as the sample project contact who submitted the water samples to SPL Kilgore. The permit application itself lists Carolyn Wood (Sr. Environmental Regulatory Engineer) and Katy Groom (Manager, Environmental Regulatory Affairs) as the SpaceX contacts for technical and administrative matters respectively; however, these individuals do not appear directly in the CoC forms — only Rodolfo Longoria does. [Citation: SPL Kilgore Project 1105141, CoC Print Group 001 of 001; SPL Kilgore Project 1106094, Table of Contents]

query: ¿Qué contaminantes se esperan en las descargas de la instalación según el resumen en lenguaje sencillo?

tool_input.query: contaminantes esperados descargas instalación resumen lenguaje sencillo español

reference: Según el Resumen en Lenguaje Sencillo (Plantilla en Español), se espera que las descargas de la instalación Starbase Launch Pad Site contengan: sólidos disueltos totales, nitrógeno-nitrato, fósforo, sulfato, cloruro, fluoruro, aluminio, cadmio, cromo, cobre, zinc y cianuro. Las aguas residuales industriales serán tratadas mediante reutilización del agua de diluvio y pretratamiento según sea necesario. [Cita: TCEQ-20972 — Plantilla en Español, Resumen en Lenguaje Sencillo, Páginas 1–2]

query: What laboratory performed the analytical testing and what accreditations does it hold?

tool_input.query: laboratory SPL accreditation NELAC analytical testing water samples

reference: The analytical testing was performed by SPL, Inc. — Kilgore laboratory, located at 2600 Dudley Rd., Kilgore, Texas 75662 (with a regional office at 2401 Village Dr., Suite C, Brownsville, TX 78521). Unless otherwise noted, SPL Kilgore holds International, Federal, and state accreditations. Results covered by the lab's NELAC (National Environmental Laboratory Accreditation Conference) scope are marked (N)ELAC in the reports; parameters outside that scope are marked with 'z'. [Citation: SPL Kilgore Project Reports 1105141 and 1106094 — Qualifier and accreditation footnotes]

query: That answers all my questions, thanks!
reference: You're welcome! If you have any more questions about the SpaceX TPDES permit application or the Starbase testing report, feel free to ask.
